title: CommandCenterUI v2: chatroom UX + native lab-wrapper window
description: Operator feedback on the released TASK-086 app: (1) it is not really a standalone program -- it should be a wrapper for the AI Command Center Lab, i.e. its own application window, not a browser tab; (2) communications UX is terrible -- the composer demands recipients/intent/identity form-filling before sending; it must feel like a chatroom. Build: chat-first UI reading the live hcom message stream and agent presence directly from ~/.hcom/hcom.db (read-only), a one-box composer where Enter sends, @mentions parsed from the text, no-mention messages broadcast, default intent inform; sends attributed to the operator's established external identity 'command-center' via hcom send --from (no setup friction, no agent impersonation); a native GTK/WebKit window wrapper so it launches as its own program (firefox fallback); MAP task/event state kept as a secondary rail. Keep localhost-only binding and same-origin POST guard; PROMO-0004 security second pass applies.
acceptance_criteria: App launches as its own window (GTK/WebKit wrapper, firefox app-window fallback), not a plain browser tab
Chat feed shows live hcom messages (sender, text, time, mentions) and agent presence, updating automatically within a few seconds
Sending a message requires exactly: type text, press Enter -- no recipient/intent/identity form fields; @name in text targets agents, otherwise broadcast
Sends appear in hcom attributed to the operator identity command-center (external), never to an agent identity
Server stays 127.0.0.1-only with the same-origin POST guard intact; hcom.db opened read-only
